data.title: Barratt Redrow's forward sales drop 10%, maintains homebuilding target
data.description:
UK homebuilder Barratt Redrow said on Wednesday its forward sales - a key gauge of near-term demand - were 10.1% lower at March-end from a year earlier but reiterated its target of building between 16,800 and 17,200 homes this year.

Mounting concerns about the outlook for the UK and the global economy amid a tariff war have further undermined confidence of potential homebuyers already struggling with elevated borrowing costs and sticky inflation.

The company said its private home order book stood at 5,503 homes as at March 31, 3.5% lower than a year ago. However, the value of the order book was 3.3% higher due to modest inflation and a change in sales mix.

data.title: Heineken's Q1 Revenue Falls; Outlook Affirmed
data.description:
Heineken , said Wednesday that revenue declined year over year in the first quarter ended March 30.

The Dutch brewer's revenue was 7.78 billion euros, compared with 8.18 billion euros previously and the FactSet-compiled consensus sales estimate of 6.50 billion euros.

Looking ahead, the company reiterated its guidance of a 4% to 8% organic growth in operating profit for 2025.

data.title: Afry Enlisted as Engineering Partner for Swedish Medicinal Production Facility
data.description:
Afry said it was selected as the engineering partner and project manager for CCRM Nordic's planned production facility for advanced therapy medicinal products in Mölndal, Sweden.

The engineering, design, digital and advisory services company said in a Wednesday release that it will plan and design the facility, which is expected to be completed in the second quarter of 2026.

data.title: Antofagasta Posts Higher Q1 Copper, Gold Production
data.description:
Antofagasta logged a rise in copper and gold production in the first quarter and maintained its full-year copper production outlook.

Copper production totaled 154,700 tonnes, up from 129,400 tonnes a year ago, and gold production came in at 42,900 ounces, compared with 33,300 ounces, according to a Wednesday filing.

The miner's molybdenum production was 3,100 tonnes, compared with 2,700 tonnes a year ago.

Meanwhile, Copper sales in the three months jumped to 170,200 tonnes from 115,700 tonnes.

Antofagasta maintained its full-year group copper production guidance of 660,000 tonnes to 700,000 tonnes.

data.title: DXN Signs First Data Centre as Service Agreement with US Satellite Provider
data.description:
DXN secured its first data center as a service agreement with a US-based satellite provider to design, build, and deploy prefabricated infrastructure at an initial site in Northern Territory, according to a Wednesday filing with the Australian bourse.

The five-year contract, valued at AU$3.6 million, includes an upfront payment of AU$600,000, and recurring revenue for ongoing maintenance and management services, the filing said.

Shares of the company rose 17% on market close.

data.title: Market Chatter: Chinese AI Startup Zhipu to Launch IPO by October
data.description:
Chinese artificial intelligence startup Zhipu plans to list by October this year, Bloomberg News reported Tuesday, citing documents filed with the Chinese stock market regulator.

The company, backed by Alibaba and Tencent , had earlier rolled out AI services that it said could rival ChatGPT.

Zhipu has reportedly tapped CICC to manage its IPO.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Yonz Technology Launches 100-Gigawatt Solar Frame Project
data.description:
Yonz Technology started construction of its 100-gigawatt photovoltaic aluminum frame production base in Inner Mongolia, China, after the groundbreaking ceremony held Tuesday.

The 3.5-billion-yuan project includes manufacturing lines, warehouses and a 600,000-ton aluminum alloy smelting facility, according to a Wednesday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

Shares of the aluminum frame manufacturer rose less than 2% in recent trade.

data.title: India's IREDA jumps on higher quarterly profit, Phillip Capital raises to 'neutral'
data.description:
** Shares of Indian Renewable Energy Development Agency (IREDA) rise as much as 7.5% to 179.50 rupees, highest since February 21

** The renewable energy financier on Tuesday reported 48.7% Y/Y increase in fourth-quarter profit, 40.1% Y/Y rise in interest income

** Phillip Capital see strong loan growth for IREDA at a CAGR of ~23% over FY25-27 on rising demand for renewable energy in the country

** However, brokerage says higher exposure to private sector and high proportion of vulnerable portfolio do not provide confidence for low credit costs in the medium term

** Upgrades stock to "Neutral" rating from "Sell", maintains TP at 150 rupees

** More than 68.9 mln shares change hands, 3.5x of 30-day avg

** Stock last up 5%, trimming YTD loss to 18.5%

data.title: Shares skid in Asia as Nvidia curbs fan worries, gold surges to record
data.description:
Key points:
  • Nvidia slumps 6% after hours as Washington restricts chip sales to China
  • Wall Street futures fall, traders await Powell speech
  • Gold climbs 2%, Swiss franc jumps 1%

Shares fell in Asia on Wednesday as AI darling Nvidia took a hit from U.S. curbs on chip sales to China, highlighting the damage to come in a tit-for-tat global trade war, while gold hit a record and the safe-haven currencies jumped.

Treasury yields were steady ahead of a key speech from Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell later in the day. Traders are wondering if he will echo the surprisingly dovish tone set by his colleague Fed Governor Christopher Waller, or stay more balanced.

Overnight, Washington issued new export licensing requirements for Nvidia's H20 and AMD's MI308 artificial intelligence chips to China. Nvidia's shares slumped 6% in after-hours trading, after it said the move would cost $5.5 billion.

"This disclosure is a clear sign that Nvidia now has massive restrictions and hurdles in selling to China," said Daniel Ives, analyst at Wedbush Securities.

"The Street will take this news with clear nervousness worried these are the first shots fired in the tech battle between the US and China and Beijing/Xi are not just going to take this news and walk away."

Separately, President Donald Trump ordered a probe into potential new tariffs on all U.S. critical minerals imports, on top of reviews into pharmaceutical and chip imports. Beijing is continuing to play hardball, having reportedly ordered airlines to suspend deliveries of Boeing aircraft .

The sell-off in Asian stocks gathered pace in the afternoon. S&P 500 futures fell 1.5% while Nasdaq futures slumped 2.3%.

The gloom is set to spread to Europe, with EURO STOXX 50 futures pointing to a 1.5% drop at the open.

On Wednesday, MSCI's broadest index of Asia-Pacific shares outside Japan (.MIAPJ0000PUS) fell 1.4%, snapping a four-day winning streak. Japan's Nikkei dropped 1.6%.

Chinese blue chips fell 0.7% as investors failed to find much solace in some solid GDP data that predated the tariff increases in April. Hong Kong's Hang Seng slumped 2.7%.

"Both countries seem to believe they have the upper hand, potentially prolonging the current stalemate for months to come," said analysts at PGIM Fixed Income in a note to clients.

"China appears to have no intention of climbing down from its current stance on tariffs and instead views the current trade dynamics as an opportunity to make inroads with countries that export to the U.S."

The White House said Trump is open to making a trade deal with China but Beijing should make the first move.

GOLD SHINES

All of the uncertainties left gold in an unstoppable position, with the bullion up 2% to hit another record high of $3,290 per ounce.

ANZ on Wednesday updated their forecast for gold to hit $3,600 an ounce by the year-end, arguing that the risk-off purchases for the asset are yet to pick up.

The step down in risk appetite is evident via rises in the Japanese yen and Swiss franc. The dollar lost 1.1% to 0.8145 Swiss franc and fell 0.7% to 142.32 yen .

Bank of Japan Governor Kazuo Ueda told the Sankei newspaper that the central bank may need to take policy action if U.S. tariffs hurt the Japanese economy, signalling the potential to pause the bank's rate-hike cycle.

U.S. Treasuries, however, failed to draw support from the risk aversion and were steady on Wednesday.

The benchmark 10-year yield was steady at 4.325%, well off the recent high of 4.592%. The 30-year yield (US30YT=RR) was little changed at 4.777%, also some 25 basis points lower from the high seen last week.

Oil prices were lower. Brent fell 1.1% at $63.99 a barrel, while U.S. crude also dropped 1.1% to $60.65 per barrel.

data.title: PREVIEW: India's Wipro nudges higher ahead of fourth-quarter results
data.description:

** Analysts on avg expect WIPR to report a 1.9% rise in Q4 rev to 226.21 billion rupees ($2.64 billion), their biggest jump in five quarters - as per data compiled by LSEG

** Q4 profit seen growing 17.8% to 33.38 billion rupees - data compiled by LSEG

** Stock down ~19% YTD, the least in IT index, which is down ~24%

($1 = 85.6425 Indian rupees)

data.title: Delorean Raises AU$7 Million in Debt Financing to Add Infrastructure to South Australia Plant
data.description:
Delorean secured AU$7 million of debt funding for the renewable gas, renewable liquid carbon dioxide, and liquid fertilizer upgrade and expansion of the SA1 bioenergy plant in South Australia, according to a Wednesday Australian bourse filing.

The upgraded plant is now fully funded and is expected to produce six different monetization streams. The firm will add infrastructure to produce renewable natural gas, renewable food-grade carbon dioxide, and liquid fertilizer, the filing said.

The additional debt is being provided by Delorean's existing debt provider, Tanarra. It carries a cash interest rate of the bank bill swap bid rate plus 6% per annum. Its capitalized interest rate is 5% per annum capitalized quarterly.

data.title: Heineken Backs Outlook After Beating Market Views
data.description:
(06:00 GMT) *Heineken 1Q Rev EUR7.78B
(06:00 GMT) *Heineken 1Q Org Rev Growth BEIA Up 0.9%
(06:00 GMT) *Analysts Saw Heineken 1Q Org Net Revenue BEIA Decrease 0.6%
(06:01 GMT) *Heineken 1Q Beer Org Volume Down 2.1%
(06:01 GMT) *Analysts Saw Heineken 1Q Beer Org Volume Down 2.9%
(06:01 GMT) *Heineken 1Q Premium Beer Volume Organic Growth 1.8%
(06:01 GMT) *Heineken Backs 2025 View
(06:01 GMT) *Heineken Sees 2025 Org Oper Pft BEIA Growth Between 4%-8%
(06:02 GMT) *Heineken: Performing in Line Despite Volatile Consumer and Geopolitical Trends
(06:03 GMT) *Heineken: Beer Volume Decline Reflects Calendar-Related Factors, As Anticipated
(06:03 GMT) *Heineken: Vietnam, India, and Ethiopia Delivered Promising Volume Growth
(06:04 GMT) *Heineken: On Track to Achieve EUR0.4 Billion Gross Savings Target for 2025
(06:17 GMT) Heineken Backs Outlook After Beating Market Views

By Michael Susin

Heineken backed its full-year guidance after quarterly performance beat market expectations, but warned that the consumer and geopolitical backdrop remains volatile.

The Dutch brewer on Wednesday said it continues to expect adjusted operating profit, which strips out exceptional and other one-off items, to grow organically between 4% and 8% in 2025.

"As the year progresses, we will be navigating a macroeconomic environment increasingly in flux, requiring us to stay agile and proactively adapt to changing circumstances," Chief Executive Dolf van den Brink said.

The target remains in line with analysts' 5.8% forecast, according to a company-compiled consensus.

For the first three months of the year, Heineken's revenue was 7.78 billion euros ($8.78 billion) compared with 8.18 billion euros a year earlier. On an adjusted basis, revenue grew organically 0.9%, beating consensus estimates of a 0.6% decrease.

Beer volume fell 2.1% on year compared with market expectations of a 2.9% drop. The drop was driven by a high comparison base and other technical factors such as fewer business days and the timing of Easter and Vietnam's Tet celebration, as previously anticipated by the company.

Write to Michael Susin at michael.susin@wsj.com

data.title: UK's Annual Retail Prices Up 3.2% in March
data.description:
The UK retail price index rose 3.2% year over year in March, following a 3.4% increase in February, data from the Office for National Statistics showed Wednesday.

The reading is in line with market expectations.

On a monthly basis, the index was 0.3% higher, against the prior 0.6% rise and the consensus estimate of a 0.4% jump.

data.title: Eildon Capital Extends Loan Facility
data.description:
Eildon Capital Group has extended its mezzanine loan facility for the Kings-Newport Village project in Victoria by six months to Sept. 30, according to a Wednesday filing with the Australian bourse.

The group also agreed to a revised interest rate of 16.5% per annum, compounding monthly, and a requirement for an AU$1.5 million repayment by June 30, to reduce the project's debt, the filing said.

data.title: U.K. Inflation Eases as Tariff Turmoil Pushes Bank of England Toward Rate CutUpdate
data.description:
By Joshua Kirby

Annual inflation eased for a second month in a row in the U.K., teeing up a new cut to interest rates as policymakers grow increasingly worried about the economic hit from U.S. President Trump's changeable tariff policy.

The consumer-price index rose 2.6% in March from a year earlier, slowing from an annual rate of inflation of 2.8% in February, the U.K.'s Office for National Statistics said Wednesday. Inflation came in a little lower than economists had expected, according to a consensus of estimates compiled by The Wall Street Journal.

Annual services inflation, a key focus for policymakers at the Bank of England, eased for the first time this year, reaching 4.7% from 5.0% in the first two months of 2025.

Inflation is still running higher than it has for most of the last year, underscoring the extent of the Bank of England's challenge in taking inflation to its 2% target and keeping it there. The BOE has since last year cut interest rates at a slower pace than both the U.S. Federal Reserve and the European Central Bank, held back by the stronger price pressures affecting the British economy. The BOE expects inflation to begin to rise from April as a result of an increase in household energy prices, as well as higher payroll taxes and an increase in the national minimum wage.

"Taken together, we think the [BOE's] monetary policy committee will judge this as broadly in line with its forecast and therefore viewed as evidence that inflation in March was on track, before the significant shock to the outlook that has occurred since April 2," wrote Jack Meaning and Iaroslav Shelepko, economists at Barclays.

The BOE's focus is increasingly trained on the dangers to the domestic and global economy from Washington's planned import tariffs, set out at the beginning of this month. The U.K., which doesn't run a major surplus in its goods trade with the U.S., has largely avoided President Trump's crosshairs.

But British exports are subject to Washington's blanket 10% baseline tariff, as well as a separate 25% duty on cars and metals. The U.S. is the largest single export market for the U.K. goods, and tariffs on British goods as well as indirect effects from a chillier global trade landscape, could mean GDP expanding just 0.7% this year, compared with 1.5% in case tariffs were not implemented, according to calculations by the National Institute of Economic and Social Research, a think tank.

While the hit to activity could cool inflation, some economists warn that tariffs could also drive prices higher if the U.K. government were to retaliate against U.S. measures or the pound were to weaken significantly.

How those forces interact is hard to judge at this point, BOE Deputy Governor Sarah Breeden said.

"I would expect tariffs to lower economic activity," she said. "But it's too early to call the overall impact on inflation and hence the appropriate monetary policy response."

Ahead of the data's release, investors were pricing in a nine out of ten chance the BOE would move to cut the Bank Rate by a quarter of a point to 4.25%, in what would be a fourth cut since last summer.

Domestic pressures nevertheless remain within the BOE's focus. Pay growth accelerated in the three months to February, data this week showed, keeping in relief the risk of a wage-price spiral.

Write to Joshua Kirby at joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby

data.title: Santhera Pharmaceuticals Secures Funding for Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy Drug in UK
data.description:
Santhera Pharmaceuticals announced Wednesday that the National Health Service has now approved funding for Agamree, a drug for Duchenne muscular dystrophy.

The funding will enable the Swiss specialty pharmaceutical company to begin the drug's prescription in England.

In addition, Kye Pharmaceuticals, a sub-licensee of its commercialization partner Catalyst Pharmaceuticals, secured Health Canada's acceptance for the treatment's New Drug Submission with priority review. This acceptance supports the drug's approval in Canada by the end of 2025.

data.title: UK's Bunzl lowers 2025 outlook, pauses buyback
data.description:
British business supplies distributor Bunzl lowered its 2025 forecast and paused its share buyback programme on Wednesday, citing ongoing operational challenges in North America and broader economic uncertainty.

Bunzl now expects moderate revenue growth in 2025 at constant exchange rates and sees its group operating margin slightly below 8%, down from 8.3% in 2024.

The company also said it planned to maintain debt at the lower end of its typical range to preserve financial flexibility amid economic uncertainty.

020 7011 9988, kathy.thompson@picton.co.uk

About Picton

Established in 2005, Picton is listed on the main market of the London Stock Exchange and is a constituent of a number of EPRA indices including the FTSE EPRA Nareit Global Index.

Picton owns and actively manages a £737 million UK commercial property portfolio, invested across 48 assets and with around 350 occupiers (as at 31 December 2024).

Through an occupier focused, opportunity led approach, Picton aims to be one of the consistently best performing diversified UK REITs and has delivered upper quartile outperformance and a consistently higher income return than the MSCI Quarterly Property Index since launch. 

With a portfolio strategically positioned to capture income and capital growth, currently weighted towards the industrial sector, Picton's agile business model provides flexibility to adapt to evolving market trends over the long-term.

Picton has a responsible approach to business and is committed to being net zero carbon by 2040.

For more information please visit:www.picton.co.uk

data.title: Gold's Broader Uptrend Remains Intact, Chart ShowsMarket Talk
data.description: Gold's broader uptrend remains intact, following its recent push to a new high on the daily chart, IG's Yeap Jun Rong says in an email. The daily relative strength index is rebounding off its midline, which reinforces the bullish momentum, the market strategist says. "The 261.8% Fibonacci extension (drawn from the 2018 low to the 2020 high) points to the US$3,500 level as a potential upside target to watch ahead," the strategist says. Spot gold is 1.9% higher at $3,289.80/oz. It hit a record intraday high of $3,291.84/oz on the ICE platform earlier. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Benzene prices trend lower in Asia
data.description: On Tuesday, benzene prices fell in Asia.An industry source in Asia informed a Polymerupdate team member, "Prices were assessed lower on the back of weaker energy values coupled with bearish market trends in the Asian region."FOB Korea benzene prices on Tuesday were assessed at the USD 735-745/mt levels, down USD (-5/mt) from Monday's assessed levels.CFR China benzene prices on Tuesday were assessed at the USD 750-760/mt levels, a drop of USD (-5/mt) from Monday.Meanwhile, downstream SM prices on Tuesday were assessed flat at the USD 930-940/mt FOB Korea levels.

data.title: Heineken's first-quarter sales slightly beat forecasts
data.description:
Heineken on Wednesday reported a 0.9% increase in first-quarter organic net revenues, just exceeding analysts' expectations for a slight decline even as tariff uncertainty bites.

The world's No.2 brewer had already flagged a tough start to 2025 after it cheered investors with its 2024 performance in February. This year's first quarter saw fewer trading days due to a leap year and the unfavourable timing of large events like Easter.

Heineken said this drove an anticipated 2.1% decline in beer volumes, but it sold more of its pricier labels like namesake brand Heineken, helping to offset this dip.

"Despite volatile consumer and geopolitical trends, we are performing within the range of expectations," CEO Dolf van den Brink said in a statement.

Heineken kept its full-year guidance for profit growth of between 4% and 8% unchanged despite a huge escalation in global trade tensions sparked by the U.S. since it set the forecast in February.

data.title: UK's WH Smith reports profit decline for first-half of year
data.description:
British retailer WH Smith on Wednesday reported slightly lower profit of 45 million pounds ($59.7 million) for the first-half of the year compared with 46 million pounds a year ago, as high inflation and tighter budgets led to lower passenger numbers at travel hubs.

($1 = 0.7535 pounds)

data.title: Interim Report Q1 2025 Nanologica AB (publ)
data.description:
FINANCIAL SUMMARY 

  • Net sales for the first quarter amounted to SEK 10,751 thousand (5,037)
  • The operating loss amounted to SEK 9,241 thousand (15,331)
  • Loss after tax amounted to SEK 10,171 thousand (16,734)
  • Earnings per share before and after dilution were SEK -0.12 (-0.46)
  • Cash and cash equivalents per 31 March 2024 amounted to SEK 28,348 thousand (23,600). Shortly after the end of the quarter, the company's cash and cash equivalents increased by approximately SEK 7 million as a result of incoming payments from customers.

 

EVENTS DURING THE FIRST QUARTER 

  • In January, the fifth order for NLAB Saga® with a value of approximately SEK 8.5 million was received from a recurring customer in China. Since June 2024, the customer has placed orders for NLAB Saga® with a total value of approximately SEK 18 million.
  • The end date for the loan from Flerie Invest AB has been extended to 2 July 2027 from 5 July 2025. The loan will be paid off in stages as follows:- SEK 5 million to be amortized on June 30, 2025- 1/3 of the remaining to be amortized on June 30, 2026- The remainder to be amortized on 30 June 2027

SALES RECORD FOR A SINGLE QUARTER

The first quarter of the year meant a sales record for a single quarter for the company and a good start to the new year. The ongoing positive customer dialogues combined with a continued stabilization of the production process create good conditions for increased sales growth during the year.

 

Net sales for the quarter amounted to SEK 10,751 thousand and consist mainly of sales of our silica media NLAB Saga®. In January, we received the fifth order for NLAB Saga® from a recurring customer in China, with a value of approx. SEK 8.5 million. Since June 2024, the customer who uses NLAB Saga® in the production of GLP-1 analogues has placed orders for a total of approx. SEK 18 million. We continue to work closely with this customer and believe that more orders will come from them during the year. During the quarter, we also delivered a small production order to an insulin manufacturer, as well as several evaluation orders to customers where we are working to take them from using our product on a lab scale to implement it in large-scale production.

Our team works intensively with both new and existing customers. So far, this has yielded results in China, but we are also seeing positive signals for several of our products in India. When we have several customers who are closer to buying our products, it becomes clear that they all have the same checklist, but that they may have different ways of prioritizing. They evaluate us regarding product quality, application knowledge, delivery capacity and the company's long-term approach. Customers who prioritize product quality and application knowledge rank us the highest, while those who want a supplier they have worked with before or who has a strong track record rank us lower. This means that every new deal we make builds our credibility with other customers.

We are currently experiencing strong demand, especially for the product that manufacturers of GLP-1 analogues traditionally buy. What is holding us back is that we lack sufficient volumes of stocks of that product type for the demand we are now seeing. There is also a great need for other products, but here the timing of the various projects comes into play – the customers' production schedules and internal processes are both difficult to predict and to influence for us.

During Q1, we increased resources for the work to stabilize the large-scale production of silica and implemented improvements in several process steps. These changes will have an effect at the end of the second quarter with a significant increase in output and better predictability in production, which lays the foundation for higher sales going forward. We have also made adjustments to address the need for products for GLP-1 manufacturing. The production process was initially set up so that the majority would be product types that are mainly used in insulin purification. Adjustments mean that the majority now produced are product types that are primarily used in the purification of GLP-1 analogues. 

In summary, we are confident that we will deliver growth during the year, while it is likely that sales will vary from quarter to quarter.

I get a lot of questions about our purification media NLAB® Siv. I was recently in India and had a very good dialogue with the customer to whom we delivered NLAB® Siv last year at a value of just over SEK 4 million. The customer has made major capacity investments and restructuring in their production facility with requirements for regulatory approvals as a result. This has affected their schedule and thus their ability to order more from us. They emphasize that they are satisfied with our product, which together with their capacity expansion, means that I continue to be positive that orders will come, even if I do not know when.

Last year, we developed another non-silica-based purification media, NLAB® Idun, as a complement to NLAB Saga® in the purification of peptide-based drugs. By adding NLAB® Idun to our product portfolio, we can further streamline our customers' workflows and thus reduce their production costs even more. During the first quarter, several customers have evaluated a beta version of NLAB® Idun with good results and we plan to launch the product in the second half of this year.

The world around us is turbulent right now and there is a great deal of general concern regarding access to the all-important American market. So far, the ongoing trade war has not affected our business, and the US tariffs hit the same for all silica suppliers since no one has production in the US. Our customers may become affected, but we cannot see that yet. GLP-1 drugs are in short supply, and of those in commercial production, as well as those in late-stage development, a vanishingly small proportion are manufactured in the US. To satisfy the current demand, producers outside the US will therefore be needed for a long period to come.  Of course, we continue to monitor the development closely.

Despite a shaky surrounding world, I see a bright outlook for Nanologica's development in 2025. The combination of strong global demand and our improvement and expansion of production capacity means that I expect strong sales growth in the second half of the year. I look forward to reporting on continued progress during the rest of the year.

Södertälje in April 2025 

Andreas Bhagwani, CEO

data.title: Moldova's trade gap widens 40% y/y in Jan-Feb
data.description:
CHISINAU (Moldova), April 16 (SeeNews) - Moldova's trade deficit grew by 39.9% on the year in the first two months of 2025, to $1.083 billion (954.95 million euro), as exports fell while imports rose, the statistical office said.

Exports decreased by an annual 14.8% in the January-February period, whereas imports increased by 16%, the statistical office, BNS, said in a statement on Wednesday.

In February alone, exports dropped by 17.7% on the year to $269.1 million, while imports went up by 16.3% to $875.2 million. Consequently, the trade deficit rose by an annual 42.6% to $606.1 million.

Details follow (in millions of U.S. dollars):

Jan-Feby/y change
Exports512.4-14.8%
Imports1,595.6+16.0%

Trade deficit...1,083.2...+39.9%

Source: BNS

($ = 0.8816 euro)

data.title: Playtech Names John Gleasure as Next Chairman
data.description:
By Najat Kantouar

Playtech appointed John Gleasure as nonexecutive chairman to succeed Brian Mattingley, who will step down from his role in May.

The gambling-software company said Wednesday that Gleasure will assume his new role after the company's annual general meeting.

Gleasure has 30 years of experience across the sports, media and technology sectors. He currently serves as a nonexecutive director at the sports streaming platform DAZN Group and is a nonexecutive director at the Sporting News, a global digital publisher.

Write to Najat Kantouar at najat.kantouar@wsj.com
